main.subject: a tall weathered sandstone obelisk carved with eroded ancient glyphs, sun-bleached and wind-scoured

background.ground: rolling windswept desert dunes with rippled sand and long soft shadows

sky.description: clear desert dawn, a low warm sun just breaking the horizon, pale gold to soft blue gradient, faint drifting dust haze

interaction.mood: slow reverent sunrise reveal — the obelisk emerges from the haze as the camera rises

camera.direction: low vertical hero shot that rises and arcs gently to the left as you scroll, keeping the obelisk against the dawn sun

main_image.prompt: a single tall weathered sandstone obelisk carved with eroded ancient glyphs, sun-bleached stone, wind-scoured surface, standing vertical, warm low-angle dawn rim light, seamless neutral mid-grey studio background, centered, photorealistic, ultra detailed, octane render
main_image.negative_prompt: blurry, low quality, watermark, text, people, busy background, multiple objects, snow, ice, night, cartoon

background_image.prompt: aerial three-quarter view of rolling desert dunes at dawn, rippled wind-carved sand, long soft shadows, warm gold light, faint dust haze on the horizon, photorealistic arid terrain, high detail
background_image.negative_prompt: blurry, low quality, watermark, text, buildings, people, vegetation, water, snow, night, cartoon

notes: Warm desert dawn — the tonal opposite of the Aurora Sentinel nocturne, and a check that the pipeline handles a bright scene without blowing out. timeOfDay 0.24 with a warm gold horizon fading to soft blue zenith; a low 3600K dawn key agrees with the sun just over the horizon, a cool 9000K sky fill balances the shadows, and a soft 2800K bounce warms the base. Exposure held at 0.98 and bloom restrained (0.13 @ 0.80, and the runtime's bloom gain was cut so bright skies no longer wash out) so the bright sand and sun stay premium. No stars or aurora at dawn; light dust haze via warm-tinted fog and thin clouds. A vertical 4.2 m sandstone obelisk stands on the dunes (the runtime stands vertical-silhouette heroes upright); the camera rises from a low hero framing and arcs left to sweep the obelisk across the rising sun.
